title: Whering AI outfit quality is its weakest point — explainable looks are AWEAR wedge
summary: Multiple independent sources describe Whering Dress Me/AI looks as near-random: "not much better than grabbing clothes at random", "disjointed from your personal style", "basic combinations rather than intelligent recommendations based on color harmony". No competitor shows WHY a look works.
evidence: Indyx comparison + 2026 roundups (beautyai/aurelle) + stylewithingrace tester review
proposal: Make look explainability a visible differentiator: in AI stylist / Todays Look, show why-this-works chips (color harmony, occasion, weather) next to the % match. Additive to existing Todays Look card.

title: Cataloging friction is the category make-or-break; Whering wins with auto bg-removal, loses on tag errors
summary: Whering top praise = auto background removal + auto-tagging ("removes the background & resizes the photos"); top frustration = tagging errors ("quite frustrating when Whering gets it wrong") and upload glitches. SCAN onboarding quality decides retention in this category.
evidence: whering.co.uk homepage + Indyx + review sentiment aggregate
proposal: Benchmark AWEAR SCAN flow vs Whering: auto bg-removal + auto-tag + one-tap tag correction UX; define and measure time-to-first-10-items as the SCAN onboarding metric.

title: Weather-aware daily outfit suggestion is table stakes — AWEAR has the infra, not the wiring
summary: Whering planner suggests daily outfits based on wardrobe + weather. AWEAR already has weather infrastructure (per in.md) and a Todays Look occasion engine (pickOccasionForNow) — weather is not wired into it.
evidence: 2026 roundups on Whering planner; AWEAR in.md source catalog notes weather infra exists
proposal: Wire existing weather data into pickOccasionForNow so Todays Look adapts to real weather (rain/heat) in addition to day-of-week + hour. Additive, no rework of the shipped card.

title: Whering reputation dragged by crashes/glitches — reliability is a differentiator in this category
summary: Review sentiment: "waaay too glitchy", "actually unusable" after updates, crashes during upload, slow performance. The category leader is beatable on quality bar alone; a crash-free/perf gate on mobile releases converts this into durable advantage.
evidence: Review sentiment aggregate (justuseapp/kimola snippets via WebSearch; direct fetch 403)
proposal: Adopt a crash-free / performance gate before mobile releases (Metro bundle + smoke flow on closet upload). Process change, fully reversible.

title: Whering monetization: free core, reported ~GBP 4.99/mo premium, brand contests; sustainability questioned
summary: Homepage says free to manage and style your closet; one 2025/26 roundup reports premium at ~GBP 4.99/month (UNVERIFIED vs official source). Brand collabs referenced (Prada, Gucci, Coperni, Vivienne Westwood). Indyx flags "the user is the product". Claims 7M users. Relevant price anchor for any future AWEAR premium (e.g. AI-look quota).
evidence: whering.co.uk + Indyx + roundup aggregate; App Store fetch rate-limited (429)
proposal: Founder decision: use ~GBP 4.99/mo as the freemium price anchor when defining AWEAR premium (AI-look quota already parked in IDEAS #35). Strategic/economic — not for scout to act alone.

title: Whering has no closed commerce loop — AWEAR BUY/EARN is unoccupied ground
summary: Indyx: no integrated resale marketplace in Whering; one roundup mentions a Vestiaire Collective integration (CONFLICTING, unresolved). Neither source shows in-app purchase or seller-earn loops. AWEAR already shipped /api/orders + preloved commission — this is the positioning flank.
evidence: Indyx comparison vs roundup mention of Vestiaire — conflict documented in doc
proposal: Founder-level positioning: lean marketing/product narrative on the closed loop (scan->match->look->buy preloved->earn) that Whering does not close. Strategic/brand — not for scout to act alone.

title: Receipt-forward closet import is the capture bar Alta set
summary: Alta populates the closet via garment photos, purchase-receipt forwarding, and item-DB search (TechCrunch), cutting SCAN friction to near zero for new purchases. AWEAR scan-only capture will look dated in diligence.
proposal: IDEAS: post-demo build of forward-purchase-email auto-add; mention as roadmap slide in investor demo.

title: Item-tap % closet match is uncontested by both funded AI-stylist leaders
summary: Neither Alta (mixes user-picked shopping items on an avatar) nor Style DNA (filters shops by color/body profile) scores a specific external item against the users actual closet. AWEAR WOW mechanic has no direct comp among the leaders.
proposal: IDEAS: add one-slide competitive matrix to demo narrative positioning Alta/StyleDNA as single-player closets vs AWEAR social-entry match loop.

title: Scan/cutout quality is the top churn complaint at incumbent and Altas headline promise
summary: Style DNA top review complaint is auto-cropping that cuts/splotches garments; Alta counters with snap-a-photo-get-studio-quality. Item-image quality at SCAN decides first-session trust in this category.
proposal: IDEAS: scan flow keeps original photo + manual crop-adjust fallback; make clean cutout a visible demo beat.

title: Per-look why-it-works line: nobody ships it (Alta none evidenced, StyleDNA profile-level only)
summary: Alta shows no evidenced per-look rationale; Style DNA justifies only at profile level while users complain about 4-item pairing caps. Confirms and strengthens the existing rationale-chips idea from the Whering run (INS-20260704-001).
proposal: Reinforces existing IDEAS entry (rationale chips next to percent match, INS-20260704-001) — evidence now spans 3 competitors; no new IDEAS entry needed.

title: Style DNA IAP ladder proves willingness to pay for one-off style reports
summary: Style DNA charges 7.99-19.99 USD/mo plus one-off 9.99/12.99 USD analysis reports at 4.3 stars / 7.3K ratings while Alta is still free. Paid one-off style-report SKUs are a validated revenue add-on in this category.
proposal: IDEAS: reserve a style-report SKU in the roadmap; base money model unchanged per PRODUCT_VISION (affiliate/dropship + resale fee).

title: Alta community browse is one release away from a shoppable feed — timing risk
summary: Alta already has community look browsing, avatar try-on and 4000 brand partnerships with 11M USD raised; wiring these into a tappable feed is an obvious next move. AWEAR social-entry moat has a shelf life.
proposal: Frame urgency in investor narrative (funded leader is single-player TODAY); follow-up intel check on Alta release notes ~2026-08-30.

title: Resale-value-before-you-buy is the proven hook — upgrade the Depop-at-50% row into a value-retention signal
summary: Phia users most praise seeing future resale value before purchasing (slows impulse buys, builds trust). AWEAR item sheet shows a flat Depop-at-50% row; reframing it as "Resale value ~$X, keeps ~Y%" turns a cheaper-alternative row into an asset-value signal feeding EARN.
proposal: IDEAS: item-sheet resale row reframe (valentino); done-area per OW-011 so founder-gated, not INBOX

title: Phia ($35M Series A) roadmap adds closet-based outfits + sell features — BUY-side attacker converging on AWEAR loop
summary: TechCrunch Jan-2026: Phia (valuation ~$185M, 6200 retail partners, 100Ks MAU, 11x revenue growth) plans personalized outfit recs from closet contents and selling/donating features. With Alta attacking from the closet side, AWEAR is approached from both ends; social item-tap match loop remains uncontested (no social layer at Phia).
proposal: Extend the existing competition-matrix deck slide (INS-20260705-002) to a two-front convergence framing

title: Google-Flights price-band verdict (fair/typical/high) is the category UX for buy-to-source
summary: Phia core loop: one-tap Should-I-Buy-This -> overpriced/typical/fair verdict + new-vs-resale price graph, from 250M listings across 40K sites; no published accuracy methodology. A one-word fair-price chip next to From-$X in AWEAR buy options is demo-safe with static data.
proposal: IDEAS: fair-price chip on buy options, secondary to resale-value reframe

title: Affiliate-only model fundable at $185M — validates AWEAR locked BUY monetization; deck ammo
summary: Phia monetizes purely via affiliate cut; brands report +15% AOV / +30% new-customer acquisition / -50% return rates. Market: US secondhand apparel +14% in 2024, $74B by 2029, 59% would shift secondhand under tariff price rises. Direct validation + citable numbers for the fundraising narrative.
proposal: Deck ammo noted in doc + IDEAS convergence entry; no product change

title: Doctrine: no off-app capture — Phia HTML-capture incident shows the trust downside
summary: Nov-2025: Phia browser extension captured HTML of visited sites, removed after discovery; reviews flag extension permission fears. AWEAR closet-first capture avoids this privacy surface entirely — keep it that way.
proposal: Doctrine recorded in doc; no action

title: $11/user/month contribution is off ~20-40x for the affiliate phase
summary: Honest math (8% blended fashion affiliate, 27% reversals, 6.5% attach, $85 AOV, Model-A credits) yields $0.20-0.40 revenue/MAU/month and ~breakeven contribution after $0.25 Claude-API COGS. $11 is only defensible per TRANSACTING user ($2.98/attributed order). Deck must be reframed before diligence.
proposal: Replace deck metric with $2.98 net/attributed order + ~80% GM + staged path to $1-2/MAU/month

title: AWEAR locked 15% resale commission is now above-market: Depop 0%, Vinted buyer-pays ~5%
summary: Depop removed its 10% US/UK seller fee entirely; Vinted (the only profitable comp, EUR 17.8M on EUR 596M) charges sellers 0% and buyers ~5%+EUR0.70. Poshmark 20% take never produced sustained profit. The one-click-listing-from-digital-closet premium is the only justification for 15%.
proposal: Founder review of 15% structure at Phase 3 (options incl. buyer-pays hybrid) — flagged as founder question, not changed

title: StyleDNA proves category willingness-to-pay $7.99-19.99/mo — subscription flips AWEAR contribution positive
summary: StyleDNA (4.3 stars, 7.3K ratings) monetizes closet-AI via freemium; Whering reportedly ~GBP4.99/mo. An AWEAR Pro at $5.99 (scan always free) at 3-5% conversion covers system-wide Claude-API COGS and is the first structurally positive margin layer.
proposal: Approve AWEAR Pro in principle for Phase 2 (post-raise); accelerate only if API cost/MAU exceeds $0.40

title: Paid CAC ($20-50/activated user) can never pay back on affiliate-only LTV ($2-4)
summary: Shopping CPI $2.5-6.2 in NA divided by ~40% activation and realistic retention gives $20-50 per active user, vs affiliate-only LTV of $2-4 at 15% monthly churn. LTV/CAC >4x is achievable only via the staged engine with organic CAC ($3-7).
proposal: Zero paid acquisition until Pro/resale layers live; $18K marketing budget goes to founder-led content + barter credits

title: Ads engine ceiling: Pinterest global ARPU $7.21/YEAR ($30.84 US+CA) — Phase 4 only
summary: Pinterest global ARPU of $0.60/user/month after 500M+ MAU and a full ads org proves sponsored placement is not a pre-scale revenue answer, and benchmarks how aggressive $11/user/MONTH was.
proposal: Defer any ads/sponsored work until 5M+ MAU; use as deck credibility anchor
